subject: Science
chapter: Exploring Substances: Acidic, Basic and Neutral

activities_projects.title: Quantitative Titration Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Determine the concentration of an unknown acid via neutralization
steps: Measure a fixed volume of acid and add indicator
Slowly add base until a permanent pale pink color appears
Record the volume of base used
Use the ratio to calculate the relative strength of the acid
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the stoichiometric point of a chemical reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of titration and accuracy of calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Metal-Acid Reaction & Hydrogen Test
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the reaction between dilute acids and metals
steps: Add zinc granules to the acid in a test tube
Observe the effervescence and collect the gas
Bring a burning matchstick near the mouth of the tube
Record the 'pop' sound and write the chemical equation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies hydrogen gas as a byproduct of acid-metal reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful gas identification and balanced equation writing (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Carbonate Neutralization & CO2 Verification
activities_projects.objective: Study the reaction of acids with metal carbonates
steps: React the acid with sodium carbonate in a flask
Pass the evolved gas through lime water
Observe the lime water turning milky
Explain the formation of Calcium Carbonate precipitate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the formation of salt, water, and CO2 from carbonates
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct interpretation of the lime water test (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Heat of Neutralization Measurement
activities_projects.objective: Determine if neutralization is an exothermic or endothermic process
steps: Measure the initial temperatures of the acid and base
Mix them in an insulated cup
Record the maximum temperature reached
Calculate the temperature change ($ \Delta T $)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes that neutralization is an exothermic reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in thermal data collection and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Synthetic vs. Natural Indicator Sensitivity
activities_projects.objective: Compare the sensitivity and pH range of different indicators
steps: Test each indicator against a known pH scale
Document the exact pH at which the color transition occurs
Create a comparison chart of 'endpoint' accuracy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates why specific indicators are chosen for specific reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed comparative chart and logical conclusions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Corrosion Rate of Different Acids
activities_projects.objective: Compare the 'strength' of organic vs. inorganic acids
steps: Weigh two identical iron nails
Submerge one in each acid for 48 hours
Clean, dry, and re-weigh the nails
Calculate the percentage of mass lost to corrosion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between strong and weak acids based on reactivity
activities_projects.evaluation: Data-driven report on corrosion rates (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Soil Remediation Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the amount of 'lime' needed to treat 'acid rain' affected soil
steps: Test the initial pH of the acidic soil
Add small, measured amounts of lime and re-test pH
Determine the 'Neutralization Factor' (mass of lime per kg of soil)
Propose a scale-up plan for a 1-acre farm
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies chemical neutralization to environmental engineering
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility and accuracy of the remediation plan (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Chemistry of Effervescent Tablets
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the components of a dry acid-base mixture
steps: Explain why the tablet does not react while dry
Introduce water and measure the volume of gas produced (via water displacement)
Identify the acid and base components within the tablet
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand the role of water as a medium for ionic reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific paper on the 'Solid-State Stability' of the tablet (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Electrolytic Decomposition of Salts
activities_projects.objective: Reverse a neutralization reaction using electricity
steps: Set up an electrolysis cell with Copper Sulphate
Observe the deposition of copper on the cathode
Test the remaining solution's pH over time
Explain why the solution becomes more acidic
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the relationship between salts, ions, and pH shifts
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate observation of the electrolytic process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Research Project: Industrial Neutralization
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how factories treat chemical waste
steps: Select an industry (e.g., Textiles or Leather Tanning)
Identify the specific acids/bases used in their process
Diagram the 'Effluent Treatment Plant' (ETP) process
Present the chemical equations used in the waste treatment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects classroom chemistry to large-scale industrial safety
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and technical accuracy of the presentation (10 marks)
